text: This movie was so bad that it actually became the highlight of my entire week.
So bad it was entertaining enough to save the whole night.
The game was awful in a way that made it the funniest part of my night.
The acting was terrible, but somehow it made the show unforgettable in the best way.
I had high hopes for the steak, but it tasted like a flip-flop that had been left in the sun for three days.
I expected dinner to be amazing, but the pasta tasted like wet cardboard.
I was excited for the app, but it crashed before I could log in.
The trailer looked promising, but the movie felt like two hours of waiting for something to happen.
Oh great, another software update. Because I definitely did not have enough bugs to deal with already.
Great, another password reset because my account definitely needed more problems.
Amazing, another delay, just what I needed today.
Fantastic, the checkout failed again and now my cart is empty.
The service was not exactly what I would call subpar, but it was not particularly impressive either.
The presentation was not bad, but not memorable either.
Not exactly terrible, not exactly good.
The product is not awful, but it is not impressive enough to recommend.
The sequel makes the original look like a masterpiece of cinematic history.
This new version makes the old broken version look polished.
The replacement makes the original seem flawless.
The redesign makes last year's clunky layout look elegant.
